item.title: Truck driver, 33, charged over Guildford train crash that injured three people
item.description: The 33-year-old driver is accused of driving dangerously and driving with an illicit drug in his system in a crash that injured three people.

item.title: Star Swan takes MOTY contender as Crows notch vital win
item.description: The Adelaide Crows beat the Swans in a thriller in the wet at the SCG to jump into the top four.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Andy Burnham to become next UK PM after British-Australian candidate gets one vote
item.description: Andy Burnham will be Britain's seventh prime minister in 10 years next week after winning a Labour leadership contest in which his only challenger, an Australian, received just one vote.

item.title: Ukraine destroys strategic bomber aircraft at Russia's Engels airbase, Zelenskyy says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the military plane was used by Russia for missile strikes on Ukraine.

item.title: Cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers dies aged 89
item.description: Sir Don Bradman once described Sobers as "the greatest all-round cricketer I ever saw".

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Sam Neill's Marvel cameo, 70s punk rock and numbat tongues
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Australia's newest medical drama The F Ward, starring Ioane Sa'ula, mixes Bump with Grey's Anatomy
item.description: Growing up, Ioane Sa'ula thought he'd probably play rugby league professionally, but an injury scuppered his plans. Now, he's the romantic lead of new Aussie medical drama, The F Ward.

item.title: Battery installation rates collapse as 'solar-coaster' strikes back
item.description: Federal subsidies have ignited huge demand for batteries, but a tightening of the rules has experts warning of "boom and bust" cycles for the industry.

item.title: Oil-rich Gulf nations building alternatives to Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Amid uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz, for months Gulf countries and oil companies have been finding ways to bypass the choke point.

item.title: Shortage of refined fuels threatening global economy
item.description: There is a rapidly escalating supply crunch that has yet to show up on oil markets. While the price of crude has risen in the past week as the fragile Iran war ceasefire has crumbled, the remarkably restrained price moves have helped create a false sense of security.

item.title: One Nation's Malcolm Roberts falsely claimed COVID-19 vaccines caused miscarriages
item.description: The One Nation senator made the previously unreported remarks during a live stream discussion with three prominent Australian anti-vaccine mandate activists.

item.title: From scream king to COVID dad: The on and off-screen faces of Sam Neill
item.description: The world has been remembering Sam Neill's incredible film and television career in the wake of the actor's death, but his off-screen antics left a robust impact.

item.title: Want to know who you'll marry? Science says you should look in the mirror
item.description: Once I saw it, I couldn't unsee it. Had I noticed something real or was it just the people around me? I set out to find out what the data had to say.

item.title: Prospects for Palestinian statehood 'disappearing', former world leaders say
item.description: The two former world leaders are demanding the international community heap more pressure on Israel to change course.

item.title: Federal racism inquiry hears of racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence
item.description: Racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence have taken a toll on First Nations people, a federal inquiry heard this week.

item.title: Minister concedes big tech companies using Australian content to train AI models is 'unethical'
item.description: Australian artists have already had their music taken to train overseas AI models without payment or consent.

item.title: Protests scrutinised as antisemitism royal commission focuses on university activism
item.description: Universities have apologised to Jewish staff and students who have spoken of their distress at being targeted by protest movement.

item.title: Platform connects city investors with farming across Australia via their smartphones
item.description: An Australian investment platform is helping farmers raise capital by connecting them with everyday investors who can buy shares in crops and livestock while sharing the risks and rewards of farming.

item.title: Sub-Antarctic petrels spotted live off NSW coast for first time
item.description: Two Southern Ocean deepwater seabirds, the Kerguelen petrel and the grey petrel, have been photographed off Port Macquarie, during a "stunningly rare" southern seabird event.

item.title: Queensland government crime statistics compare 'apples to oranges' amid timeframe inconsistency
item.description: Queensland's premier says new statistics show crime is falling across the state, but his data sources are inconsistent.

item.title: New report paints mixed picture of Sydney's rental market
item.description: The June quarterly rent report from Domain paints a mixed picture of Sydney's market, including both major surges and significant drops for rent prices in certain suburbs.

item.title: Artist behind portrait in Epstein files reunites with collector who purchased first piece
item.description: A high school art portrait launched Petrina Ryan-Kleid's career. Forty years and several satirical portraits later, she has been reunited with the person who bought her first work.

item.title: Attacking the media for asking legitimate question is straight out of Trump's playbook
item.description: A minister of the Crown's scolding of a journalist asking a legitimate question about firearm ownership, and the subsequent pile-on allowed to run on social media, should ring alarm bells for anyone concerned with democracy.

item.title: Winter weather brings new and unique shells to southern WA beaches
item.description: WA's southern coastline is covered in seashells, much to the delight of collectors. However, the state has a bag limit on how many you can take home.

item.title: How a century-old town hall became the heart of a small Tasmanian community
item.description: Every Sunday, a group of men and women get together for some American clogging, home-baked afternoon tea and community connection.

item.title: Victoria on high alert for signs of deadly H5N1 bird flu
item.description: Victorians are on high alert for the deadly H5N1 bird flu, as citizen scientists help to monitor infected birds.

item.title: Young Australians with bowel cancer say doctors missing the warning signs
item.description: Georgie Cauchi had blood in her stool, constant pain and was struggling to get out of bed, but still in her 20s doctors told her she was too young to have bowel cancer. When it was finally discovered several years later, the tumour had grown so large it was blocking her entire bowel.

item.title: Harp therapist serenades lemurs, rhinos and fish at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium
item.description: Harp therapist Alison Ware provides musical enrichment for animals at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium, adapting what she plays to suit different species.

item.title: Irish national admits role in 'nightmare' plan to help alleged criminal flee Australia
item.description: After agreeing to help smuggle an alleged drug trafficker out of Australia, Stephen Roche found himself at the centre of a living "nightmare", stranded in the outback without funds or friends, court documents have revealed.

item.title: Bridge destroyed in Iran's Bandar Khamir after US strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tasmanian government may have to pay $200m for Liberty Bell Bay remediation
item.description: The clean-up bill for the Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter site may ultimately may be paid by the Tasmanian government, according to legal experts, following administrators' failure to secure a buyer for the embattled operation.

item.title: What comes next after the landmark BHP strike in Port Hedland?
item.description: For the first time in more than two decades, BHP workers in the the Pilbara went on strike. What do we know about how much impact it had and what comes next?

item.title: How far can you go when defending your home against an intruder in NSW?
item.description: A suspected home invasion in northern NSW that left one man shot and an elderly couple with stab wounds sparks renewed discussion about self-defence and so-called "castle law".

item.title: Australian government warns about risk of methanol poisoning and crime in Laos
item.description: Authorities say tourists should exercise a high degree of caution in the country due to the risk of methanol poisoning, which killed Australian women Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles in 2024.

item.title: This dal is a quick, simple and satisfying winter warmer - ABC News
item.description: Lentils are having a moment and this easy dal is an incredibly easy way to make the most of them.

item.title: China sitting on huge stockpiles of frozen beef as new tariffs bring imports to a halt
item.description: As imports from Australia hit a wall because of large tariffs, China turns to its historically high stockpile of frozen beef.

item.title: Lettuce at Taco Bell in five US states confirmed as a source of 'explosive diarrhoea'-causing parasite
item.description: A record number of cyclospora cases have been reported in more than 30 US states, but experts say not every illness is likely to have been caused by a single source.

item.title: Pies get home against Blues, Lions survive Eagles scare
item.description: Collingwood completes a 21-point win over rivals Carlton, as Brisbane is forced to work for a 24-point victory over West Coast.

item.title: Activists fight to preserve historic Frank Street home on Lismore flood plain
item.description: There is a push to save precious memories stored within the walls of an historic home on Lismore's flood plain.

item.title: Olympians return to iconic North Sydney pool for 50-year reunion of Montreal games
item.description: Members of the 1976 Australian Olympic swim team return to North Sydney Olympic pool to celebrate their 50-year reunion and revisit the venue that shaped their careers.

item.title: India reopens one of world's oldest nuclear power plants and that's just the beginning
item.description: Despite repeated setbacks and isolation, India continues to hold onto its nuclear dream.

item.title: 'It's not right': Dragons fume after pushing Warriors
item.description: Second-placed New Zealand Warriors squeak to a nervy 20-12 victory over wooden-spoon favourite St George Illawarra, although the Dragons fume over narrow refereeing calls.

item.title: Regulating AI is like catching smoke, warns Christopher Pyne
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Failed' university scheme hurting home ownership prospects
item.description: There are calls for the government to dump a scheme that has more than doubled the cost of some degrees.

item.title: Perth medical researchers working on artificial placentas for premature babies
item.description: The aim of the technology is to give babies born too soon a few more weeks to grow and develop in womb-like conditions, and a better chance of a long and healthy life.

item.title: 'Jimothy', a raccoon with short spine syndrome who roams Seattle, gains cult following online
item.description: A raccoon with a rare birth defect has amassed a legion of fans after a video of it darting across a footpath and running up a set of stairs racked up more than 6.8 million views in just three days.

item.title: Deformed raccoon gains cult following after online videos go viral
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wallabies thrash Italy as Schmidt era ends in triumph
item.description: The Wallabies record a convincing 57-10 victory over Italy in Perth, as Joe Schmidt's coaching tenure finishes on a winning note.

item.title: India's Skyroot takes off in first private orbital rocket mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Eight killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian targets
item.description: Two sprawling warehouses of Russia's major online retailer are hit by Ukrainian drones, local officials say, with massive plumes of smoke towering over a facility at Elektrostal, 50 kilometres east of Moscow.

item.title: Police in India remove activist amid hunger strike for education reform
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Indian activist on 20-day hunger strike forcibly taken to hospital after examination scandal affects millions
item.description: Sonam Wangchuk, who began a hunger strike last month in solidarity with India's youth party after an examination scandal affected millions of students, was forced to go to hospital by court order due to his "deteriorating health condition".

item.title: 'Lack of evidence' means Lao officials won't lay charges related to deaths of two Australians
item.description: Investigators in Laos gave one of their first detailed accounts of the investigation into the deaths of six people, including 19-year-old Australians Holly Morton-Bowles and Bianca Jones, saying they don't have enough evidence to lay charges because autopsies were not carried out.

item.title: The Lemon Twigs on meeting Brian Wilson, making new album Look For Your Mind!
item.description: These New York twenty-somethings make music indebted to Brian Wilson, Todd Rundgren and the Fab Four. But do The Lemon Twigs get sick of the comparisons?

item.title: How 'expansive masculinity' is helping boys become healthy men
item.description: These men are being the role models they never had. They hope to curb Australia's masculinity crisis.

item.title: Quiet change to Nauru's policing laws could impact NZYQ legal challenges
item.description: Advocates question the timing of law changes that coincide with mounting federal court challenges from individuals fighting deportation.

item.title: As Europe and Canada follow Australia's social media ban for young people, the US moves another way
item.description: More and more countries are following Australia's lead and introducing social media bans for children, but US policies are piecemeal and vary from state to state.

item.title: Indonesian corruption investigators discover stash of gold bars in hidden safe
item.description: Indonesian police investigating suspected corruption discover millions of dollars worth of currency and 74 kilograms of gold in a safe hidden in a luxury house near Jakarta.

item.title: How the World Cup trophy was designed and why the Jules Rimet is no longer awarded
item.description: It has become the most iconic trophy in world sport, and it all started in a small design studio in Milan more than 50 years ago.

item.title: Pauline Hanson risks losing everyday Australians while busy with the richest
item.description: Many voters are unhappy with the hand they feel successive governments have dealt them and no-one has tapped into that better than Pauline Hanson.

item.title: Biohacking and anti-aging science are raising hopes of living longer. What's hype and what shows promise?
item.description: The fear of aging is driving a wave of biohacking businesses promising to help restore youth and fight disease. Four Corners investigates the race to reverse the aging process.

item.title: Liberty Justice Center, the conservative law firm that took on Donald Trump and won
item.description: When a law centre decided to fight Donald Trump's "liberation day" tariffs, it was acutely aware of possible "retaliation" from a president who takes matters "personally".

item.title: US military launches new air strikes to 'swiftly punish' Iran for deaths of US troops
item.description: The US and Iran have intensified attacks since an interim ceasefire deal signed a month ago fell apart, raising the possibility of a return to all-out war.

item.title: Cattle farmers plant trees for sustainable aviation fuel
item.description: A north Queensland farming family is trialling planting biofuel-producing trees alongside their cattle.

item.title: Everything to know about Exercise Pitch Black as war games arrive in Top End
item.description: Military aircraft and aviators from 20 countries land in Darwin ahead of Exercise Pitch Black 2026, with the biennial war games poised to begin.

item.title: Jazz festivals canned in Newcastle and Victoria over payday superannuation
item.description: Two long-running Australian jazz festivals have been cancelled, with organisers blaming new superannuation rules.

item.title: Barmah sand slug fix a complex headache due to competing demands
item.description: The Murray Darling Basin Authority is finalising its study on what to do about an increasing river impediment amid a First Nations call to find common ground.

item.title: Inquiry hears of CFMEU's political influence and intimidation in Queensland
item.description: The inquiry heard claims former senior public servants and industry figures faced aggressive union tactics.

item.title: Small bars revitalise Sydney nightlife as special entertainment precincts expand
item.description: Taran Howard opened up his small bar in Sydney's inner west last September. Since then, he has learnt how to mix drinks "on the fly".

item.title: Afghan women's refugee cricket team celebrates ICC funding, pathway to internationals
item.description: Five years since fleeing Afghanistan after the Taliban returned to power, the country's women's refugee cricket team has secured a pathway to compete at the international level.

item.title: Energy reliability scheme is adding to power bills, SA government admits
item.description: A new SA government energy scheme set to collect almost $44 million from electricity users this financial year has been described by a critic as a "hidden charge".

item.title: Giant earthworms found in SA 'almost certainly' a new species
item.description: A South Australian ecologist has been tracking giant earthworm sightings for 30 years, despite the species not officially existing in the state.

item.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
item.description: British prosecutors say they are seeking the extradition of the Tate brothers from the US on rape and sex trafficking charges.

item.title: Fishers frustrated by new zoning restrictions in WA marine park
item.description: New zoning rules for a marine park off WA's far north are being rolled out, introducing fishing restrictions and penalties.

item.title: Iran shares video claiming attacks launched on US targets
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aussie Leo with growth hormone deficiency dreaming to star at World Cup like Messi
item.description: Lionel Messi was diagnosed with growth hormone deficiency, which is a rare condition, when he was younger. He has become a role model for young Leo Slattery from Brisbane.

item.title: China cracks down on AI companions, forcing millions to break up with virtual partners
item.description: A boyfriend betrayed 17-year-old Xiaoxue, so she made an AI partner. But now the government is trying to end her relationship.

item.title: Future of AI
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: AI regulation in political cartoons with Mike Bowers and Tina Quinn
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Interview with Andrew Bragg
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Researcher says pesticides could be harming Carnaby's black cockatoos
item.description: Research has found pesticide levels "well above" maximums set for humans and animal feed, in some cases hundreds of times higher than set by regulators.

item.title: Mitch McConnell's absence from the US Senate reignites debate about age limits
item.description: Washington's rumour mill is hyperactive at the best of times. But an elderly senator's lengthy absence from Congress is cranking it into frenzied overdrive.

item.title: Senator Andrew Bragg defies Liberal president Tony Abbott's 'failed' multiculturalism claim
item.description: Senator Andrew Bragg says he "strongly" disagrees with the recently elected Liberal Party president, who claims diversity is "not a strength".

item.title: WA's north has questions over policies implemented by outgoing MP Paul Papalia
item.description: Residents in WA's north have been left with a slew of unanswered questions relating to crime and justice in the wake of long-serving WA government minister Paul Papalia's resignation.

item.title: Love story: Why these best friends plan on being buried side by side
item.description: Tania Differding and Ella Crofts met as teenagers and have been inseparable since. The best friends, now aged 43, are planning the rest of their lives around one another.

item.title: The oil crisis may depend on China: here's why
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Moment Andrew Tate was arrested and handcuffed in Miami
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hinkley set to coach Devils, Caddy taken to hospital as Dons end drought
item.description: Tasmania prepares to unveil Ken Hinkley as its inaugural AFL coach as Essendon snaps a 12-game losing streak despite losing Nate Caddy early.

item.title: Bek left a doomsday church at 27. Here's what her life looks like now
item.description: Author Bek Condello says she has not spoken to her friends or family inside the church since she decided to leave.

item.title: Tom Trbojevic gutted after another injury, Titans, Dolphins record wins
item.description: The Dolphins shook off last week's humiliating thrashing to power past the Cowboys 36-16 at Lang Park, after Tom Trbojevic suffered another hamstring injury in Manly's defeat to Gold Coast.

item.title: Emu Run Tours sues vehicle manufacturer over fatal 2022 bus crash outside Alice Springs
item.description: An Alice Springs tour company is suing a vehicle manufacturer for negligence and breach of contract following a bus crash that killed a 69-year-old man in 2022.

item.title: Actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90 after more than half a century on Australian screens
item.description: Jason Donovan has paid tribute to his father who has died at the age of 90 after decades on Australian screens.

item.title: Secret Harbour by-election: Meet the candidates vying for your vote
item.description: The Secret Harbour by-election is shaping up as one of the most closely-watched political contests in WA for some time. Here's everything you need to know about the candidates and how to vote.

item.title: World Cup: Argentina fans stage staggering Times Square takeover ahead of final against Spain
item.description: Times Square has been the epicentre for World Cup celebrations throughout the tournament, but it reached new heights as Argentina fans swamped the "centre of the universe" ahead of the final against Spain in nearby New Jersey.

item.title: Store-bought vodka from licensed IGA retailer found to contain key ingredient in paint stripper
item.description: Australia's bootleg booze market is booming and an alarming new discovery from a bottle of vodka sold in Melbourne reveals it is turning into a major health problem.

item.title: Hundreds of activists join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of Pine Gap protest
item.description: Hundreds of activists and academics have gathered in Alice Springs to join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of a planned protest against the joint Australian-US military base at Pine Gap.

item.title: Special guest dazzles aspiring young astronauts at Adelaide's Space Day Out
item.description: Young South Australians got one step closer to achieving their space exploration dreams on Sunday when they met a "real-life" astronaut.

item.title: Russia bombards Kyic with ballistic missiles
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Russia unleashes one of biggest ballistic missile attacks of the Ukraine war, Kyiv says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia battered Ukraine's capital Kyiv overnight into Sunday local time, with one of its biggest ballistic missile barrages of the war.

item.title: Federal government to tighten rules around use of AI in departmental decision-making
item.description: Agencies like Centrelink and Services Australia will have to follow a framework to ensure decision-making is "fair and accurate" if they use AI.

item.title: Israeli military says Iranian missiles launched toward Jordanian city of Aqaba
item.description: The US warns its citizens to stay away from air and sea ports at Aqaba in southern Jordan, hours before Israeli authorities announced that Iranian missiles had been launched towards the city.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she is 'not backing away' from Tommy Robinson interview
item.description: The One Nation leader was a guest on the podcast of controversial far-right UK figure Tommy Robinson, where she lamented the end of the White Australia policy.

item.title: World Cup final ends in glory for Spain and heartache for champion Messi
item.description: Spain wins the 2026 World Cup, with Ferran Torres coming off the bench to score the winner in extra time against 10-man Argentina.

item.title: This week in history quiz, July 20: Test your smarts on the first Moon landing, John Howard and the Riot Act
item.description: Consider yourself a history buff? Test your smarts on the first Moon landing, John Howard and the Riot Act.

item.title: Race Around the World recap: Catch the week seven films
item.description: From Mexican wrestling to Indian slums, catch up on every week seven film from Race Around the World.

item.title: How you can get the balance right when being online is your job
item.description: When being across trends in your industry feels like part of your job it can feel like work never ends. Here's how to protect your free time.

item.title: Telehealth peptide prescriptions expose longevity industry stretching the rules
item.description: The booming industry selling Australians on treatments to live longer is exploiting significant gaps in health regulation, exposing consumers to risk.

item.title: Humanoid robot markets expand as Australia reckons with AI revolution
item.description: Australia this week made an important step forward in grappling with the AI revolution. Meanwhile, in Melbourne a company is making humanoid robots.

item.title: Tony Abbott isn't helping the Liberals with their Pauline Hanson problem
item.description: Pauline Hanson's grievance politics is a challenge for Labor, but a splitting headache for the Liberals — compounded by former prime minister Tony Abbott.

item.title: ABC Your Say invites people across the country to tell us what you think
item.description: Sulaiman Zandinan says Australia is amazing. Wendy Newman says people in the bush are "not country bumpkins". The ABC is inviting you to have your say about the issues, challenges and opportunities that shape your life in 2026.

item.title: Doctors worry health system can't make up for loss of services if Hobart Private sells to Catholic-run Calvary
item.description: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

item.title: Paramedics told to take complex cardiac patients to Launceston General Hospital instead of Mersey
item.description: Paramedics have been told to transport complex cardiac patients in Tasmania's north-west to the Launceston General Hospital rather than the local Mersey Community Hospital.

item.title: Council of Europe questions World Cup's integrity after controversial 2026 tournament
item.description: Europe's top human rights body hits out at FIFA over the integrity of the World Cup and calls for change before the 2030 tournament.

item.title: Lesbian couples considered separate families in updated Queensland Health fertility clinic guidance
item.description: Queensland Health advice, given to fertility providers to help navigate the state's assisted reproductive technology legislation, has had a "devastating" impact on some same-sex couples trying to have more children.

item.title: Queensland set for classic winter week, hazardous surf in south-east to weaken
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology says showers from the Wide Bay to the Gold Coast are possible today, with hazardous surf conditions forecast to weaken.

item.title: World Cup winners Spain will receive championship rings, but almost 2,000 of them will be bought by the public
item.description: World Cup winners Spain will receive championship rings, but so will almost 2,000 people who have enough money to buy them.

item.title: Play goes beyond the archives to revive an Adelaide street's lost history
item.description: It's been a three year labour of love probing into the past and building on the fragments of a hidden history to write Logan St and get it to the stage.

item.title: The wait for unbuilt social housing felt as Sydney's homeless 'still out on the street'
item.description: People with lived experience of homelessness say the issue is deepening as housing stock remains out of reach and community providers are left struggling to cope with demand.

item.title: Hundreds of Queensland school support staff lodge complaints about violence
item.description: About four hundred staff — the majority teacher aides — have complained to the United Workers Union since April about facing violent behaviour from students and parents.

item.title: Government urged to rethink $1.4b Western Sydney airport road upgrade
item.description: Some transport planners say the design of a major Western Sydney road upgrade is "nonsensical" and is at odds with the government's commitment to public transport and new housing.

item.title: Migrant DFV victim-survivors made to prove genuineness of relationship
item.description: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

item.title: Ryan Fox takes out British Open as Lucas Herbert is denied at the death
item.description: Ryan Fox shows nerves of steel to claim the British Open as Australia's Lucas Herbert has a near miss.

item.title: ASX slips, oil jumps, markets remain on edge as war and AI worries drag down sentiment — as it happened
item.description: The ASX edged lower as markets fret about war and inflated AI valuations. Look back on how the session unfolded on our markets blog.

item.title: Jonas Vingegaard out of Tour de France after fracturing collarbone in 'devastating' crash
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard's Tour de France campaign comes to an end after a crash on the 15th stage.

item.title: Taylor responds to Liberal's praise for Malcolm Roberts — as it happened
item.description: Angus Taylor distances himself from a Liberal senator's endorsement of One Nation's Malcolm Roberts.

item.title: ASIC sounds alarm on Australians' exposure to growing risks in private credit
item.description: The corporate regulator is concerned about the risks posed by private credit for Australians with superannuation.

item.title: Endangered bettongs reintroduced in WA
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Former Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley announced as Tasmania Devils inaugural coach
item.description: The former Port Adelaide coach is formally announced as the inaugural coach of the Tasmania Devils. It completes a year-long hunt for a senior coach for the AFL's newest club.

item.title: 'Rat kangaroos' reintroduced to Dirk Hartog Island as researchers get hands dirty
item.description: Researchers were unafraid to get their hands "dirty" in an ambitious bid to restore WA's largest island to its pre-colonial state.

item.title: What to know about the Tate brothers facing rape and trafficking charges
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami after UK prosecutors brought further rape and sex trafficking charges against them. Here's what we know about the arrests and extradition request.

item.title: What is the offside rule? Why Spain's goal was disallowed in the World Cup final
item.description: Wondering why that Ferran Torres goal in extra time of the World Cup final was disallowed? We're here to help.

item.title: Football Victoria condemns Preston Lions fans over conduct at South Melbourne game
item.description: A professional football club in Melbourne's north faces calls to ban hardline supporters who brandished sexist and "ethnically derogatory" banners at a match on Saturday.

item.title: US launches more strikes on Iran and IRGC attacks oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US launches a new round of air strikes on Iran after announcing the death of another American service member, and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has immobilised two oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: World Cup final quick hits: Argentina lose heads in defeat, Trump crashes Spain's party
item.description: Argentina's players lash out in defeat, Ferran Torres becomes a Spanish icon forever and Donald Trump attempts to crash the winners' party. Here are the World Cup final quick hits.

item.title: WNBA star Angel Reese accepts Sandy Brondello's apology over 'protected species' comment
item.description: Angel Reese says she is overwhelmed by the support and response after comments made by the Opals coach on the sidelines.

item.title: Victoria Premier Jacinta Allan says 'witch' billboard drives disrespect for women
item.description: The Victorian premier says a controversial billboard seen around Melbourne again is "dangerous" and driving disrespect for women.

item.title: Jennifer Finch, bassist for grunge rock feminists L7, dies at 59
item.description: Grunge trailblazers L7 honour their "beloved bandmate, friend and fellow troublemaker", who died just five days after revealing she had been diagnosed with aggressive brain cancer.

item.title: Is AI changing your life in unexpected ways? Should we move faster or more cautiously? The ABC wants to hear from you
item.description: Most of us never chose to live in an artificial intelligence (AI) world. Yet over the past few years, it has become part of everyday life, changing how we think, learn and communicate.

item.title: Australia-India export deal triggers debate over uranium opportunities in Broken Hill
item.description: Australia's trade deal to supply India with unenriched uranium sparks conversations about potential new mining opportunities in Broken Hill.

item.title: AI wildlife warning system trial aims to reduce animal-vehicle collisions on Monaro Highway near Canberra
item.description: An estimated 10 million animals are killed on Australian roads each year. It's hoped a new artificial intelligence-powered alert system being trialled on the Monaro Highway near Canberra will reduce the toll.

item.title: Essendon star Nate Caddy cleared to face Hawthorn despite abrupt hospital visit
item.description: Nate Caddy is expected to fully train and play next weekend despite experiencing an elevated heart rate during Essendon's win over GWS.

item.title: Derryn Hinch's brother remembers 'colourful, constantly changing' life — as it happened
item.description: Derryn Hinch, the outspoken and at times controversial broadcaster and former senator, is being farewelled at a state funeral service in Melbourne after his death earlier this month, aged 82.

item.title: 13 photos that sum up the chaos of the 2026 World Cup final between Spain and Argentina
item.description: It was magical chaos from start to finish as Spain claimed victory in the 2026 World Cup final. Here are the 13 photos that summed it up.

item.title: Key takeaways from random FIFA World Cup half-time show
item.description: Superstar performers at the inaugural FIFA World Cup half-time show included Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira and BTS, with Australian Emmanuel Kelly part of the fold.

item.title: Woman in serious condition as police investigate stabbing in Kalgoorlie
item.description: Part of the mining city's main street was blocked off and detectives are investigating after the woman was found with serious stab wounds.

item.title: Tears streaming, Lionel Messi departs as a legend despite World Cup final defeat
item.description: As the champion wept on the pitch after the World Cup final defeat, Argentina may well have wondered with trepidation what life will look like in the post-Messi era.

item.title: Baby Thea Flaskett's death at Redcliffe Hospital caused by congenital heart defect, inquest finds
item.description: Thea Flaskett died four hours after she was born via vacuum assisted delivery at Redcliffe Hospital in September 2023.

item.title: US military releases footage it claims shows strikes against Iranian targets
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Research video shows gorilla laughing after being tickled
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Labor MP Mark Dreyfus shares experiences of antisemitism with royal commission
item.description: Former attorney-general Mark Dreyfus has pushed for more robust social media moderation and protest laws at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

item.title: Brisbane woman naturally conceives identical quadruplets in 'one of the rarest births in the world'
item.description: A Queensland family has grown by four after an extremely rare pregnancy with identical quadruplets all sharing one placenta.

item.title: Maclean's fishing co-op site to be sold amid white spot disease fallout
item.description: A seafood outlet that has been operating for almost 80 years on the NSW Mid North Coast will go up for auction as the Clarence River Fishermen's Co-op battles financial challenges posed by white spot disease regulations.

item.title: As individual stars lit up World Cup, Spain's triumph a reminder collective spirit trumps all
item.description: As some of football's biggest names lit up the World Cup, Spain flew under the radar all the way to hoisting the trophy on its final day.

item.title: Albanese dashes hopes for new NT hospital during $10m maternity announcement
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces $10 million to improve maternity services at Royal Darwin Hospital, but maternity care advocates say the money is unlikely to improve staffing issues.

item.title: Man admits to making 'menacing' phone call to Age journalist Nick McKenzie
item.description: David Scholes, 32, has been ordered to complete an anger management course after he was caught making a "menacing" phone call to award-winning journalist Nick McKenzie.

item.title: Ken Hinkley on excitement at being named Devils coach
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Derryn Hinch remembered for sense of justice, outspoken and likeable personality at state funeral
item.description: Broadcaster Ray Martin, Australian actress Jacki Weaver and performer Rhonda Burchmore are among the entertainment heavyweights to pay tribute to Derryn Hinch at his state funeral service.

item.title: Greens accuse AirTrain of ripping off Queensland taxpayers
item.description: Financial reports from a company that owns the Brisbane AirTrain service show it paid zero income tax between 2015 and 2025 financial years.

item.title: Trial begins for UK YouTuber Yung Filly accused of violent sexual assault of young fan in Perth
item.description: A Perth court hears disturbing details of the YouTube personality's alleged sexual assault of a young woman who had a "celebrity crush" on him.

item.title: North Queensland school stabbing accused released on strict bail, victim discharged from hospital
item.description: The magistrate says the bail application brought by the boy's lawyer has "weakened" the police case that the stabbing was premeditated, and that the boy was the aggressor.

item.title: Four easy one-pot dinners that are perfect for winter
item.description: From butter beans with jammy leaks to a quick chicken soup, these recipes are for you if you're on hunt for a low-effort, tasty weeknight meal.

item.title: Australia's Bernard Tomic wins first Challenger Tour title in eight years
item.description: Veteran tennis player Bernard Tomic continues to toil on the world circuit and is rewarded with his first Challenger Tour title in eight years.

item.title: 'ISIS bride' Rayann El Houli granted bail by Melbourne court
item.description: A woman facing terror charges after living under Islamic State rule in Syria will be freed on bail, after being in custody for two months.

item.title: Imalu Tigers, Tapalinga Super Stars crowned premiers at 2026 Tiwi Islands football grand finals
item.description: The Imalu Tigers and Tapalinga Super Stars are the new premiers of Tiwi Islands football, claiming victory in the men's and women's competitions in electric grand final matches on Sunday.

item.title: Author Randa Abdel-Fattah sues Bendigo council, La Trobe University over Bendigo Writers Festival
item.description: Randa Abdel-Fattah launches legal action after Bendigo Writers Festival organisers sent a code of conduct that sparked censorship concerns and led to the boycott of dozens of authors.

item.title: Telehealth ban still standing between dying patients and their doctors
item.description: A federal law which threatens doctors if they use electronic communications, such as the internet or phone, to discuss voluntary assisted dying remains in force, even though telehealth is now an established part of healthcare.

item.title: Mount Barker restaurant owners Wok the Duck launch petition against government's land acquisition
item.description: The owners of a Mount Barker restaurant have launched a petition to block the government's proposed acquisition of the site on which their business stands.

item.title: SA's largest electorate to be renamed in honour of Aboriginal leader
item.description: South Australia's largest federal electorate will be renamed in honour of an Aboriginal leader. Experts say it is also the state's most likely to fall to One Nation.

item.title: Tribunal upholds three-match ban for Crows forward Darcy Fogarty
item.description: The Crows fail in their challenge to Darcy Fogarty's three-match striking ban at the AFL Tribunal, as the panel finds Fogarty had time to decide to swing his arm at Sydney's Harry Cunningham. Follow live.

item.title: Man found guilty of murdering two-year-old boy at Coffs Harbour
item.description: A jury heard the boy was rushed to hospital after he was found with a catastrophic brain injury and a fractured pelvis and rib.

item.title: LGBTQIA+ nightclub's lease at former Sydney church terminated due to 'offensive trade'
item.description: Organisers of Divine Playhouse were served a breach notice earlier this month, in the wake of complaints from some members of the Christian community.

item.title: Brett McMaster avoids jail for trafficking cocaine while Australian Army soldier
item.description: The former rifleman and field medic sourced cocaine from a fellow Townsville soldier and on-sold the drugs to army colleagues.

item.title: Supporters of India's youth-led 'cockroach' movement caned by police in attempt to march on parliament
item.description: Thousands of supporters of India's youth-led "cockroach" movement gathered in New Delhi to march ‌on parliament seeking the resignation of the education minister, despite police denying permission for the protest.

item.title: Waste burning trial at Adbri cement site prompts backlash from council
item.description: A major cement manufacturer has been allowed to burn more plastic waste for fuel at its Adelaide site in a decision a local council is seeking to challenge in court.

item.title: Monday Finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Israel argues draft Labor platform gives 'insufficient attention' to Hamas
item.description: Israel's embassy has released a lengthy complaint ahead of Labor's national conference on the party's proposed position on Israel and Palestine.

item.title: Indian police beat 'Cockroach' movement protesters ahead of march
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Indian police pushing 'Cockroach' movement protesters
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Alleged murderer Johnathan Paul Parker denies killing woman in Adelaide's south
item.description: A man accused of murdering a woman in Adelaide's southern suburbs tells a court he is not to blame for her death, and instead alleges the crime was committed by the victim's ex-wife.

item.title: Dirt cheap and toxic alcohol being sold
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Hundreds march on secretive military base Pine Gap in Alice Springs, in biggest protest at site in decades
item.description: About 300 people have marched on Pine Gap in Alice Springs, protesting against its role in US military operations, in the largest protest at the top-secret defence site since 1987.

item.title: Andy Burnham becomes British PM, provides AUKUS hints in first speech
item.description: Andy Burnham has pledged to provide immediate cost-of-living relief for Brits and invited US President Donald Trump to Manchester in the hours after becoming UK PM on Monday.

item.title: Trump says Iran will 'pay' as US names two soldiers killed in Jordan
item.description: The deaths bring the number of US service members killed in the war with Iran to 17, while officials work to identify remains found where a third service member went missing.

item.title: Houthi rebels in Yemen declare maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia, escalating threat to world energy supplies
item.description: In a statement, the Houthis military spokesperson Yahya Saree said the group would enforce the blockade against its "criminal Saudi enemy" effective immediately.

item.title: Having a spouse with dementia increases your own risk of condition, study finds
item.description: Having a spouse with dementia puts you at a higher risk of developing the condition, a new study finds.

item.title: Moscow targeted by drones while Russia bombards Kyiv
item.description: Ukraine launches its latest attack on the Russian capital, firing more than 400 drones, while the Kremlin deploys more ballistic missiles against targets across Ukraine.

item.title: 2025-02-09T180247Z_1790781638_RC23RCAV1DL3_RTRMADP_3_BRITAIN-POLITICS-FARAGE
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man charged with murder following death of UK politician Ann Widdecombe
item.description: Joshua Kerry, 28, will appear in court on Tuesday, UK time, after the death of the prominent conservative politician in her home earlier in July.

item.title: 'Narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home, studies show. But are they right?
item.description: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

item.title: The extraordinary career of cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason, beyond performing at the royal wedding
item.description: Sheku Kanneh-Mason went viral performing at Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding. He's captivated audiences since and is touring Australia this month.

item.title: Australias kids' TV quiz, part 1: Do you remember these shows?
item.description: What was the name of the tough, mangy cat in The Ferals? And which show featured EC, the faceless doll?

item.title: How some Chinese EVs can have a new battery installed in about four minutes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chinese-made autonomous ‘flying cars’ to hit the market this year
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Owning an EV is now cheaper than owning a petrol car in China
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Once an 'embarrassment' China's EV industry now leads the world
item.description: In China you can swap an empty electric vehicle battery in about four minutes. It is part of how China has come to dominate the EV industry.

item.title: Inquiry into violence against sex workers to examine stubborn stigma and legal solutions
item.description: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

item.title: Prepare for stock market pain as China throws a spanner into AI boom
item.description: Elon Musk's latest venture sent global stock markets to dizzying heights, but its performance since does not augur well for the company or the future of the AI boom.

item.title: Property market transforming with cold feet, cooling prices and withdrawals
item.description: Global instability, shifting tax policies and interest rate pressures force buyers and sellers to adjust to the changing property landscape.

item.title: Why Anthony Albanese would 'have a disagreement' with his younger self now
item.description: From left-wing roots to "radical centre" prime minister, Anthony Albanese reflects on his 30-year career in Parliament House.
